1.gl: Ashshur, the second son of Shem; also his descendants and the country occupied by them (i.e. Assyria), its region and its empire
1.kj: Asshur, Assur, Assyria, Assyrians. See H838 (אָשֻׁר).
1.dv: or אַשֻּׁר; apparently from H833 (אָשַׁר) (in the sense of successful);

1.gl: to catch (in a net, trap or pit); generally, to capture or occupy; also to choose (by lot); figuratively, to cohere
1.kj: [idiom] at all, catch (self), be frozen, be holden, stick together, take.

1.gl: to feed on; figuratively, to consume; by implication, to battle (as destruction)
1.kj: devour, eat, [idiom] ever, fight(-ing), overcome, prevail, (make) war(-ring).

1.gl: a year (as a revolution of time)

3.gl: a signal (literally or figuratively), as aflag, beacon, monument, omen, prodigy, evidence, etc.
3.dv: probably from H225 (אוּת) (in the sense of appearing);

Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
3.kj: Jehovah, the Lord. Compare H3050 (יָהּ), H3069 (יְהֹוִה).

4.gl: to drive forth (a person, an animal or chariot), i.e. lead, carry away; reflexively, to proceed (i.e. impel or guide oneself); also (from the panting induced by effort), to sigh
4.kj: acquaint, bring (away), carry away, drive (away), lead (away, forth), (be) guide, lead (away, forth).

4.gl: Ashshur, the second son of Shem; also his descendants and the country occupied by them (i.e. Assyria), its region and its empire
4.kj: Asshur, Assur, Assyria, Assyrians. See H838 (אָשֻׁר).
4.dv: or אַשֻּׁר; apparently from H833 (אָשַׁר) (in the sense of successful);

4.gl: (concretely) a boy (as active), from the age of infancy to adolescence; by implication, a servant; also (by interch. of sex), a girl (of similar latitude in age)
4.kj: babe, boy, child, damsel (from the margin), lad, servant, young (man).

4.gl: to strip off, i.e. generally to make naked (for exertion or in disgrace), to drain away or bail up (a liquid)
4.kj: make bare, clean, discover, draw out, take, uncover.

4.gl: exiled; captured; as noun, exile (abstractly or concretely and collectively); by extension, booty
4.kj: captive(-ity), prisoners, [idiom] take away, that was taken.

4.gl: Cush (or Ethiopia), the name of a son of Ham, and of his territory; also of an Israelite

5.gl: properly, to prostrate; hence, to break down, either (literally) by violence, or (figuratively) by confusion and fear
5.kj: abolish, affright, be (make) afraid, amaze, beat down, discourage, (cause to) dismay, go down, scare, terrify.

5.gl: properly, to pale, i.e. by implication to be ashamed; also (by implication) to be disappointed or delayed
5.kj: (be, make, bring to, cause, put to, with, a-) shamed(-d), be (put to) confounded(-fusion), become dry, delay, be long.

5.gl: ornament (abstractly or concretely, literally or figuratively)
5.kj: beauty(-iful), bravery, comely, fair, glory(-ious), honour, majesty.

5.gl: Cush (or Ethiopia), the name of a son of Ham, and of his territory; also of an Israelite
Mitsrajim, i.e. Upper and Lower Egypt

6.gl: the face (as the part that turns); used in a great variety of applications (literally and figuratively); also (with prepositional prefix) as a preposition (before, etc.)

6.gl: properly, like this, i.e. by implication, (of manner) thus (or so); also (of place) here (or hither); or (of time) now

6.dv: from the prefix k and H1931 (הוּא);

6.gl: properly, to be smooth, i.e. (by implication) to escape (as if by slipperiness); causatively, to release or rescue; specifically, to bring forth young, emit sparks
6.kj: deliver (self), escape, lay, leap out, let alone, let go, preserve, save, [idiom] speedily, [idiom] surely.
